topic: Two friends both apply for the same job
raw_output:
[story 1]

Liam stared at the email, his thumb hovering over the 'delete' button before retracting it. The subject line read, "Interview Invitation: Marketing Coordinator Position." A slow, creeping coldness began to spread through his chest. He reread the body of the message, the words blurring into an incomprehensible pattern. He blinked several times, trying to force his vision to reacquire the text, but it remained stubbornly the same. A sudden, sharp intake of breath escaped him, loud enough to make the quiet coffee shop patrons glance over. He felt a peculiar lightness in his limbs, as if gravity had momentarily lessened. He pushed his chair back, the scrape of metal on tile echoing unnervingly in the sudden silence of his own head.

[story 2]

"No way," Maya whispered, her voice thin and reedy. She held the printed notification between two fingers, her knuckles white against the crisp paper. Her gaze darted from the 'Congratulations' banner to the name listed beneath it. A tremor ran through her hands, causing the paper to flutter slightly. She didn't move to pick up her phone; instead, she just sat there, frozen, her jaw slack. A strange, hollow sensation settled in her stomach, a heavy, nauseating weight that made it difficult to draw a full breath. She slowly lowered the paper, her eyes wide and unblinking, as if watching a scene unfold that wasn't quite real.

[story 3]

"Did you see this?" Ben mumbled, leaning over the shared laptop screen, his brow furrowed deep enough to cast shadows over his eyes. He reread the notification three times, his finger tracing the highlighted text with an unsteady motion. When he finally looked up, his mouth hung slightly open. A faint flush crept up his neck, and he instinctively rubbed the back of his wrist, a nervous tic he hadn't realized he was engaging in. The air in the room seemed to thicken around him. He let out a short, involuntary sound—a kind of choked exhale—before quickly clearing his throat and trying to regain his composure, though his hands were visibly trembling.
